text.title: WCAG 2.2 Compliance Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the WCAG 2.2 Compliance methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: If a help mechanism (link, contact, chat) appears on multiple pages, it MUST appear in the same relative order on each page.
Do not ask users to enter the same information twice in a session unless security re-verification requires it or the previous entry is no longer valid.
When a component receives keyboard focus, the focus indicator MUST NOT be entirely hidden by sticky headers, cookie banners, chat widgets, or modal overlays.
Pointer-input targets MUST be at least 24×24 CSS pixels (AA); ≥44×44 for AAA / mobile-recommended.
Authentication processes MUST provide an alternative that does not require cognitive function tests (memorising, transcribing, puzzles).
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-wcag-22-compliance.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
